name: Santa Eufemia – Pozoblanco Loop
description: This difficult 37.9-mile touring cycling route from Pozoblanco explores the Los Pedroches region, featuring rolling hills and historical sit
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Pozoblanco are set within the Los Pedroches region, characterized by extensive oak dehesas and traditional pastures. The area features holm oaks, cereal fields, and olive groves, creating a varied scenic backdrop. Pozoblanco is situated within the Sierra Morena mountain range, offering diverse elevations and natural features for cyclists. This landscape provides a mix of open woodlands and more challenging mountainous terrain. Best touring cycling routes around Pozoblanco The most popular touring cycling route is Sanctuary of the Virgen de Luna — circular from Pozoblanco, a 18.6 miles (30.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This route leads to a significant religious site nestled within a natural holm oak grove. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Santa Eufemia – Pozoblanco Loop, a difficult 37.9 miles (61.1 km) path. This route traverses varied dehesa landscapes, offering a challenging ride through the region's characteristic terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the El Guijo, Torrecampo y Pedroche — circular desde Pozoblanco, a 35.6 miles (57.3 km) trail leading through traditional villages and dehesa environments, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Touring cycling around Pozoblanco is defined by expansive oak dehesas, the foothills of the Sierra Morena, and ancient olive groves. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Pozoblanco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 4 reviews. More than 230 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Pozoblanco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Pozoblanco?
A: Pozoblanco offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, allowing cyclists to explore the diverse landscapes of the Los Pedroches region.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on touring cycling routes in Pozoblanco?
A: Cycling around Pozoblanco, you'll primarily experience the expansive oak dehesas, which are traditional pastures dotted with holm oaks. The routes also traverse areas of ancient olive groves and golden cereal fields. As Pozoblanco is within the Sierra Morena mountain range, you'll also find diverse elevations and a mix of open woodlands and more challenging mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate touring cycling routes for a relaxed ride?
A: Yes, Pozoblanco offers several routes suitable for a more relaxed pace. There are 5 easy routes and 28 moderate routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027179291/sanctuary-of-the-virgen-de-luna-circular-from-pozoblanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen de Luna — circular from Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt;, which is 18.6 miles (30.0 km) long and leads through a natural holm oak grove.

Q: Where can I find more challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Pozoblanco has 21 difficult touring cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027078249/santa-eufemia-pozoblanco-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Eufemia – Pozoblanco Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 37.9 miles (61.1 km) path that traverses varied dehesa landscapes with significant elevation changes. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362947753/villaharta-espiel-y-alcaracejos-circular-from-pozoblanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaharta, Espiel y Alcaracejos — circular from Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 88.6 km with over 1200 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant cultural and natural attractions. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4288368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna&lt;/a&gt;, a religious site nestled in a holm oak grove, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malos Pasos Spring&lt;/a&gt;, known for its mineral-medicinal waters. Some routes also lead through charming villages, allowing you to explore places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4667553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor De la Villa de Dos Torres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Pozoblanco?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Pozoblanco are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027179291/sanctuary-of-the-virgen-de-luna-circular-from-pozoblanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen de Luna — circular from Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363111689/el-guijo-torrecampo-y-pedroche-circular-desde-pozoblanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Guijo, Torrecampo y Pedroche — circular desde Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through traditional villages and dehesa environments.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Pozoblanco?
A: The touring cycling routes around Pozoblanco are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique dehesa landscapes, the tranquility of the open woodlands, and the variety of routes that cater to different abilities, from moderate rides to more challenging climbs through the Sierra Morena foothills.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Pozoblanco?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling in Pozoblanco. During these seasons, temperatures are generally milder, and the landscapes are vibrant, offering comfortable conditions to explore the oak dehesas, olive groves, and cereal fields without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options or connections to larger networks?
A: Pozoblanco's location within the Sierra Morena provides access to significant long-distance routes. The region is part of the GR-48 'Sierra Morena Track,' a 590-kilometer marked route suitable for mountain biking, and also connects to parts of the Transandalus, which links ancient hill towns. While this guide focuses on touring cycling, these networks indicate the potential for extended rides.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through traditional villages?
A: Yes, several routes offer the opportunity to cycle through traditional villages, allowing you to experience the local culture and architecture. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363111689/el-guijo-torrecampo-y-pedroche-circular-desde-pozoblanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Guijo, Torrecampo y Pedroche — circular desde Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt; leads through charming villages like Pedroche, providing a glimpse into the region's heritage.

Q: Are there routes with significant elevation gain for a workout?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra Morena mountain range provides diverse elevations for challenging rides. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozoviejo Square – Puerto de La Chimorra loop from Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt; feature substantial climbs, with this particular route including over 960 meters of elevation gain over 58.5 km, making it a great option for a strenuous workout.
tips: A small square where the three symbols of the town are located (which also appear on the shield) - an oak, a well and a rooster. It is said that the first house in Pozoblanco was built here - the Casa de la Viga.

This town was born on the basis of the union between the two populations: Torrefranca and Toremilano (hence the name of Dos Torres). In the Plaza de Villa there is a small candy store that is open every day. On the outskirts there is a small Hermitage of San Sebastián with a viewpoint next to it.

It is the only door that remains from the late walled enclosure of Santa Eufemia. It is a lowered horseshoe arch flanked by Gothic columns, whose ashlars are magnificently preserved, forming one of the most endearing and suggestive corners of the town.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular cycling route from Pozoblanco?
faqs.answer: The route begins and ends in Pozoblanco. You can typically find parking within the town, especially near central areas, to commence your cycling tour.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is rated as a difficult touring bicycle route, requiring very good fitness. With over 61 km and 520 meters of elevation gain, it presents a significant challenge.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Santa Eufemia circular route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bicycles. However, be prepared for potential unpaved sections that could be challenging, especially if you have narrow tires or are carrying heavy luggage.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Pedroches region can experience hot summers. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, with milder weather and beautiful natural scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Los Pedroches region?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public routes in the Los Pedroches region. However, always respect private property and any local regulations you may encounter.

faqs.question: Is the Santa Eufemia circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While cycling with dogs can be challenging on a touring bicycle route of this length and difficulty, if your dog is accustomed to such distances and you have appropriate gear (like a trailer), it might be possible. Always ensure your dog is on a leash when not actively cycling and respect local wildlife and other users.

faqs.question: What notable places can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through areas with interesting historical and cultural points. You can visit Pozoviejo Square, the Plaza Mayor De la Villa de Dos Torres, and the historic Puerta de la Villa.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly overlap with several official trails, including the Transandalus, Camino de la Santa, and Cañada Real de la Mesta, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to resupply along the way?
faqs.answer: The route connects Pozoblanco and Santa Eufemia, passing through other villages like Dos Torres. These towns offer opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, and shops for refreshments and supplies. It's always wise to carry enough water and snacks, especially between villages.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse the natural landscapes of the Pedroches region in Córdoba, Spain. Expect rolling hills, traditional villages, and potentially olive orchards, characteristic of the broader Andalusian countryside.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this route is approximately 3 hours and 53 minutes of active cycling time. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, the type of bicycle, and how often you stop to rest or explore.

waypoints.name: Plaza Mayor De la Villa de Dos Torres
